initial_program:
% count each colour
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.

% maximum count among colours
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.

% choose a nonzero colour that achieves the maximum count
1 { MaxColour : color(MaxColour) } 1 :- count(MaxColour) = maxCount.
% MaxColour must be nonzero
:- MaxColour = 0.
% there must be at least one nonzero cell
:- maxCount = 0.

% dimensions of the grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% row and column predicates (0based)
row(R)  :- n_rows(N), R < N.
col(C)  :- n_cols(N), C < N.

% rows that contain MaxColour
rowD(R) :- input(R, _, MaxColour).

% columns that contain MaxColour
colD(C) :- input(_, C, MaxColour).

% pick three distinct rows that contain MaxColour
1 { R0 : rowD(R0) } 1 :- row(R0).
1 { R1 : rowD(R1) } 1 :- row(R1).
1 { R2 : rowD(R2) } 1 :- row(R2).

% pick three distinct columns that contain MaxColour
1 { C0 : colD(C0) } 1 :- col(C0).
1 { C1 : colD(C1) } 1 :- col(C1).
1 { C2 : colD(C2) } 1 :- col(C2).

% enforce that the three rows and three columns are pairwise distinct
:- R0 != R1.
:- R0 != R2.
:- R1 != R2.
:- C0 != C1.
:- C0 != C2.
:- C1 != C2.

% output the required 33 pattern
output(R0, C0, 0).
output(R0, C1, MaxColour).
output(R0, C2, 0).
output(R1, C0, MaxColour).
output(R1, C1, MaxColour).
output(R1, C2, MaxColour).
output(R2, C0, 0).
output(R2, C1, MaxColour).
output(R2, C2, 0).

#show output/3.
